title: Induced nonlinear phase shift of forward volume spin waves in magnetic films and one-dimensional magnonic crystals
authors: Alexey B. Ustinov, Roman V. Haponchyk, Anton P. Burovikhin, Mitsuteru Inoue, Taichi Goto
abstract: A differential phase shift of a low-power spin wave (SW) induced by a high-power pumping wave co-propagating at different frequencies in perpendicularly magnetized magnetic films has been studied. We find that this effect for forward volume SWs propagating in yttrium iron garnet (YIG) films is stronger than that for surface SWs propagating in tangentially magnetized films. The results show that the induced nonlinear phase shift up to 180° takes place for pumping wave power of a few milliwatts. The phenomenon paves the way for fast and energy-efficient control of one-dimensional magnon transport.
summary: This paper experimentally demonstrates a strong, low-power induced nonlinear phase shift in forward volume spin waves propagating in YIG magnetic films. The effect is superior to surface wave counterparts, suggesting a pathway for fast and energy-efficient control of magnon transport. This work is significant for developing next-generation magnonic logic circuits.
main_problem: The study investigates the induced nonlinear phase shift of forward volume spin waves (FVSWs) in perpendicularly magnetized magnetic films and one-dimensional magnonic crystals.
main_result: The nonlinear phase shift is significantly stronger for FVSWs in perpendicularly magnetized films compared to surface spin waves, allowing a $180^\circ$ shift with pump powers as low as a few milliwatts.
method: The research combines experimental measurements using a Vector Network Analyzer (VNA) with theoretical modeling based on nonlinear damping theory.
model_or_system: The physical system involves Yttrium Iron Garnet (YIG) films, sometimes structured into 1D magnonic crystals, which are excited and measured using microstrip antennas.
key_observables:
Induced nonlinear phase shift ($\Delta\varphi_{1INL}$), amplitude-frequency characteristic (AFC), and nonlinear damping parameters ($
u_2, \varsigma_2$).
important_parameters: Pump wave power ($P_2$), film thickness (e.g., 5.7-$\mu$m, 13.6-$\mu$m), and operating frequencies relative to magnonic band gaps.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on comparing experimental data with theoretical predictions derived from nonlinear damping theory.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the experimental setup, phase vs. time measurements, AFCs for different film thicknesses, and plots showing $\Delta\varphi_{1INL}$ dependence on pump power, particularly highlighting saturation and band gap effects.
paper_structure: The paper details the experimental setup, presents measurements of the nonlinear phase shift dependence on pump power for various film geometries (bulk vs. 1D MC), and compares these results against theoretical models.
why_interesting: While focused on magnonics, the concept of nonlinear wave interaction and energy-efficient control of wave propagation shares conceptual links with nonlinear dynamics studied in quantum optics and open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
